data.content:
Here's what I found. The project has three services riddled with hard-to-mock static/ambient dependencies:

### `OrderProcessor.cs`
| Line(s) | Static call | Problem |
|---------|------------|---------|
| 18 | `Guid.NewGuid()` | Non-deterministic ID — can't assert on it |
| 20–21 | `DateTime.UtcNow` (×2) | Non-deterministic time — can't test expiry logic |
| 25 | `DateTime.Now` | Same, plus mixes `Now`/`UtcNow` |
| 26 | `File.WriteAllText` + `Path.GetTempPath` | Writes to real filesystem in business logic |
| 33 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Can't control "now" to test `IsExpired` |
| 39 | `Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable` | Reads process-wide env var — shared global state |
| 40–46 | `Directory.Exists/CreateDirectory`, `File.WriteAllText` | More filesystem I/O buried in logic |
| 48 | `Console.WriteLine` | Side-effect coupled to stdout |

### `NotificationService.cs`
| Line(s) | Static call | Problem |
|---------|------------|---------|
| 7 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Can't set "now" to test the 7-day window |
| 15 | `Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable` | Global env coupling |
| 16 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Second instance in the same method |
| 18 | `File.AppendAllText` | Writes to real filesystem |
| 24 | `new HttpClient()` | Creates its own HTTP client — can't substitute a test handler |

### `ConfigLoader.cs`
| Line(s) | Static call | Problem |
|---------|------------|---------|
| 7 | `File.Exists` | Filesystem check — needs real file to test |
| 12 | `File.ReadAllText` | Reads from real filesystem |
| 25 | `Console.WriteLine` | Side-effect |
| 32–35 | `Directory.Exists/CreateDirectory` | Filesystem I/O |
| 38 | `File.WriteAllText` | Filesystem I/O |

### Summary

The three root causes are:

1. **`DateTime.UtcNow` / `Guid.NewGuid()`** — inject a `TimeProvider` (built-in in .NET 8+) and an `IGuidProvider` so tests can control time and IDs.
2. **`File.*` / `Directory.*` / `Environment.*`** — wrap behind `IFileSystem` (e.g. `System.IO.Abstractions`) and `IEnvironment` interfaces so tests don't touch disk or env vars.
3. **`new HttpClient()`** — accept an `IHttpClientFactory` or inject `HttpClient` with a test `HttpMessageHandler`.

Would you like me to refactor these to be testable?
data.reasoningText: Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze the testability issues across all three service files.
